title: lygodium volubile var. wrightii
intro: Lygodium volubile var. wrightii, also known as Wright's climbing fern, is a rare and endangered species of fern native to the subtropical regions of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lygodium volubile var. wrightii include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions, such as wounds and ulcers. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
